# maestro-spine llms.txt

This is the public agent map for `maestro-spine`.

Use this file to orient AI coding agents, reviewers, and documentation agents inside this repository. It maps the portable methodology layer only. It intentionally excludes private operating environments, project-specific memory, live project data, and internal review artifacts.

## Start Here

- [README.md](README.md): project overview, install paths, lineage, omissions, and license.
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): tool-agnostic operating contract for production agentic work.
- [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md): Claude Code adapter for this public skeleton.

## Core Method

- [docs/operating-model.md](docs/operating-model.md): how the Sprint Spine loop runs end to end.
- [docs/verification-iron-rule.md](docs/verification-iron-rule.md): verification discipline.
- [docs/confidence-tags.md](docs/confidence-tags.md): confidence vocabulary and evidence tags.
- [docs/anti-slop-flag-list.md](docs/anti-slop-flag-list.md): common weak-output patterns to detect and reject.

## Memory And Propagation Patterns

- [docs/brain-first-lookup.md](docs/brain-first-lookup.md): source-truth lookup before answering.
- [docs/global-brain.md](docs/global-brain.md): portable pattern for curated cross-project operating memory.
- [docs/global-decision-tag.md](docs/global-decision-tag.md): `[GLOBAL]` decision propagation convention.

## Skills

- [skills/](skills/): Sprint Spine and review skill skeletons.

These skills are skeletons. Adapt invocation metadata and runtime conventions to your own agent system.

## Lineage And License

- [ACKNOWLEDGMENTS.md](ACKNOWLEDGMENTS.md): upstream lineage and adaptation notes.
- [THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md](THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md): upstream MIT notices.
- [LICENSE](LICENSE): license.
- [NOTICE](NOTICE): naming and attribution convention.

## Public Boundary

This repository contains what travels: methodology, skeletons, rules, and documentation patterns.

It does not contain:

- private memory contents
- private project data
- operator-specific runtime tooling
- internal project reviews
- private personas or prompts
- credentials, cookies, tokens, or local machine state
- live customer, job-search, social-media, or personal context

Agents should not infer missing private context. If a task requires project-specific or private information, ask the operator for the appropriate private source truth or work in the private repo where that context belongs.

## Agent Rules

1. Start with `README.md`, then `AGENTS.md`, then `CLAUDE.md` if using Claude Code.
2. Use docs and skills in this repo as portable patterns, not as claims about a live private operating system.
3. Preserve attribution to Garry Tan's MIT-licensed `gstack` and `gbrain` where relevant.
4. Do not add private local paths, private memory, credentials, internal review notes, or project-specific source data to this public map.
5. Before public release or visibility changes, run link checks, secret scans, private-context scans, and a final full-diff review.
